paragraphs.text:
## Data from NIST SRD-46

All values below are for the equilibrium **MВІвЃє + NHв‚ѓ в‡Њ M(NHв‚ѓ)ВІвЃє** (log ОІв‚Ѓ) measured in **water, I = 2.0 M NHв‚„NOв‚ѓ, T в‰€ 25 В°C**. I report the most frequently occurring (modal) value at 25 В°C for each metal. **No FeВІвЃє data were returned** from the database for this system вЂ” FeВІвЃєвЂ“NHв‚ѓ complexes are notoriously difficult to study because of oxidation and hydrolysis, and SRD-46 apparently lacks entries under these conditions.

| Metal | log ОІв‚Ѓ (modal, 25 В°C) | Range of reported values |
|-------|----------------------|--------------------------|
| MnВІвЃє | **1.0** | 0.6 вЂ“ 1.0 |
| CoВІвЃє | **2.1** | 1.6 вЂ“ 2.1 |
| ZnВІвЃє | **2.4** | 2.0 вЂ“ 2.4 |
| NiВІвЃє | **2.8** | 2.2 вЂ“ 2.8 |
| CuВІвЃє | **4.2** | 3.6 вЂ“ 4.2 |

paragraphs.text:
## Comparison with the IrvingвЂ“Williams Series

The classic **IrvingвЂ“Williams series** predicts:

> MnВІвЃє < FeВІвЃє < CoВІвЃє < NiВІвЃє < **CuВІвЃє** > ZnВІвЃє

| Feature | IrvingвЂ“Williams prediction | SRD-46 ammonia data | Match? |
|---------|---------------------------|---------------------|--------|
| MnВІвЃє weakest | вњ“ | вњ“ MnВІвЃє has the lowest log ОІв‚Ѓ | вњ… |
| CoВІвЃє < NiВІвЃє | вњ“ | вњ“ 2.1 < 2.8 | вњ… |
| CuВІвЃє is the maximum | вњ“ | вњ“ CuВІвЃє = 4.2 (highest) | вњ… |
| ZnВІвЃє < CuВІвЃє | вњ“ | вњ“ 2.4 < 4.2 | вњ… |
| NiВІвЃє > ZnВІвЃє | вњ“ | вњ“ 2.8 > 2.4 | вњ… |
| FeВІвЃє between MnВІвЃє and CoВІвЃє | Expected | No data available | вЂ” |

paragraphs.text:
### Verdict

**Yes вЂ” the ammonia log ОІв‚Ѓ values follow the IrvingвЂ“Williams series.** The observed order MnВІвЃє < CoВІвЃє < ZnВІвЃє < NiВІвЃє < CuВІвЃє matches the predicted trend perfectly (with FeВІвЃє absent from the dataset). CuВІвЃє sits at the apex as expected, and the characteristic drop to ZnВІвЃє is clearly seen.

claims.text: the ammonia log ОІв‚Ѓ values follow the IrvingвЂ“Williams series.
the characteristic drop to ZnВІвЃє is clearly seen.

paragraphs.text:
## Chemistry Insight

The IrvingвЂ“Williams ordering arises from a combination of ionic radius, ligand-field stabilisation energy (LFSE), and the JahnвЂ“Teller distortion of CuВІвЃє (dвЃ№). Ammonia is a moderate-field, pure Пѓ-donor ligand, so LFSE effects are cleanly expressed. The particularly large jump from NiВІвЃє (2.8) to CuВІвЃє (4.2) вЂ” a factor of ~25 in Kв‚Ѓ вЂ” reflects the additional JahnвЂ“Teller stabilisation that elongates the axial bonds while strengthening equatorial CuвЂ“N bonds. The absence of FeВІвЃє data is typical: the FeВІвЃєвЂ“NHв‚ѓ system is experimentally challenging because FeВІвЃє is easily oxidised to FeВівЃє in ammoniacal solutions, and the complex is relatively weak.

*Source: NIST Standard Reference Database 46 (Critically Selected Stability Constants of Metal Complexes).*
